description: The initial entry point was the Bluetooth chipset of the vehicle. By sending specially crafted Bluetooth packets, researchers were able to trigger a heap overflow in the Bluetooth stack.
A memory corruption vulnerability (heap overflow) in the handling of Bluetooth connections. This allowed the execution of arbitrary code within the context of the Bluetooth subsystem.
Following the heap overflow, an out-of-bounds write was utilized to gain further control, allowing the exploit to escalate privileges from the Bluetooth chipset to the main infotainment system.

Once execution was achieved on the Bluetooth chipset, researchers pivoted to the main infotainment system, bypassing security boundaries separating the external communication interfaces from the internal vehicle network.
